rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITIONER LING LA'S PETITION FOR RELIEF UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE 946.6
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 16, 2026, line 1. PETITIONER LING LA'S PETITION FOR RELIEF UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE 946.6. Petitioner Ling La's petition for relief under Government Code 946.6 is granted. Ms. La has alleged discrete acts of alleged actionable medical malpractice, some of which allegedly occurred less than one year before she submitted her application for leave to present a late claim. Based on review of all the papers filed on this motion, the court finds that Ms. La made her application for leave to present a late claim within a reasonable time and that her failure to present a timely claim was due to her mistake, inadvertence, and excusable neglect as a non-legally trained person unfamiliar with the intricate rules for filing a Government Code claim and her excusable ignorance of how to proceed once she learned that the health care professionals who she claims committed malpractice were City employees. The court further finds that, due to the pendency of the other case filed by Ms. La, the City is not been able to and has not shown that it would be prejudiced if Ms.La is relieved of the requirements of filing a timely claim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC ET AL VS. ARI LAW, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 16, 2026, line 11. DEFENDANT AALAEI ALI AND NILOFAR TRUST'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Aalei Ali and Nilofar Trust's joinder in Defendants Ari Law, P.C. and Ali Ari Aalaei's motion to quash is denied. Because the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction is not jurisdictional in the sense a court lacks the authority to entertain a case (see Shaw v. Superior Court (2922) 78 Cal. App. 5th 245, 258), a motion to quash is not the proper procedural vehicle to raise that doctrine. Instead, the doctrine must be raised by a demurrer or motion to stay. (Id. at 256, n. 5). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC ET AL VS. ARI LAW, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Museum Plaza, Llc, Walid Mando, Forma, Llc And Their Attorney Of Record David Allen Ericksen Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 128.5 And 128.7; Declaration Of Ali Aalaei
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 16, 2026, line 11. DEFENDANT ARI LAW, P.C.'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Museum Plaza, LLC, Walid Mando, Forma, LLC And Their Attorney Of Record David Allen Ericksen Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 128.5 And 128.7. Defendant Ari Law, P.C.'s motion for CCP 128.5 and 128.7 sanctions is denied. Ari Law has not shown that the filing of this lawsuit was made in bad faith, is frivolous, solely intended to cause unnecessary delay or was done for an improper purpose. While there are significant issues that will likely be addressed at a later time regarding the propriety of the filing of this lawsuit, because the application of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction is arguably a discretionary call based on policy considerations and not a hard and fast rule (see Shaw v. Superior Court (2022) 78 Cal. App. 5th 245, 256-257), there is a nonfrivolous argument allowing for the filing of this lawsuit. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: INDUSTRYVAULT, LLC VS. WILLIAM H. BARNES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 16, 2026, line 12. DEFENDANT WILLIAM BARNES' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Defendant William Barnes' motion for judgment on the pleadings on both causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff IndustryVault is denied as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and granted without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for breach of negotiable instrument. The first amended complaint sufficiently alleges that the statute of limitations for breach of the loan agreement was extended by the Consent document. The plaintiff's proffered construction of the Consent document is plausible and must be accepted as true on this motion. The second cause of action for breach of negotiable instrument fails because the loan agreement is not a "negotiable instrument" as that phrase is defined in Commercial Code 3104. This because the loan agreement explicitly states another "undertaking or instruction" which section 3104(a)(3) provides is inconsistent with a document being a negotiable instrument. Plaintiff has not shown, nor is the court aware of, any reasonable possibility that plaintiff could file an amended complaint to allge a viable claim for breach of negotiable instrument. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB, INC, A VS. SAVIOR HOSPICE CARE INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 16, 2026, line 13. DEFENDANTS SAVIOR HOSPICE CARE INC, TRILOCHAN SINGH'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. Defendants Savior Hospice Care, Inc. and Trilochan Singh's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The default and default judgment against the moving parties are now vacated and they must re-file their answer and cross-complaint no later than February 3, 2026, which must be accepted for filing nunc pro tunc as of June 9, 2025. Moving defendants are entitled to the relief they seek per CCP 473(d) and CRC 2.259 due to sufficient proof that they attempted to file their answer and cross-complaint on June 9, 2025 and the Court inadvertently failed to accept them for filing. Because the answer and cross-complaint were not filed due to the error of the Court, plaintiff is not entitled to any compensatory costs. This ruling is supported by California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: JOAO NUGENT RAMOS PINTO ET AL VS. MARY FONG LAU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Defendants Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 16, 2026, line 14. DEFENDANTS MARY LAU, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE, STERLING HAVEN MANAGEMENT LLC, DESERT CANYON VENTURES LLC'S Motion For Defendants Protective Order. Defendant Mary Lau's motion for protective order is denied. Due to Lau's admission that she transferred substantial assets soon after the accident, the financial information sought by plaintiffs is directly relevant to the issues in this case and thus plaintiffs' need for the information outweighs Lau's privacy rights in the information. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC RBLF FUNDING TRUST VS. PARK PLACE FINANCE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 16, 2026, line 15. DEFENDANT PARK PLACE FINANCE, LLC'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Continued to January 28, 2026 in department 501. That department hears all non-discovery motions in cases involving real property, including real property mortgages. Counsel are required to provide courtesy copies of the complaint and all moving, opposition and reply papers to department 501 no later than January 20, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
